topics: Definition of the Laplace transform; Laplace transforms of some basic forcing functions - step, exponential, ramp, sinusoidal, cosine, pulse, impulse and translated functions
Laplace transform of derivatives and integrals
Initial value theorem and final value theorem; Numerical problems and theoretical model development of a tank heater system - input-output model and its solution

Transfer functions and their general characteristics - SISO systems, general first order and second order systems; Development of transfer function models for first order systems and their general characteristics

Development of transfer function models for first order systems - continuous single tank (mass storage) system and mercury in glass thermometer system (linear systems only); Dynamics of first order systems for step and impulse input

Development of transfer function models for second order systems - multi-capacity systems (two tanks connected in series), inherently second order systems (damped vibrator), first order system in the presence of a controller
Dynamics of second order systems - general characteristics of underdamped, overdamped and critically damped systems
Numerical problems on overshoot, decay ratio, period of oscillation, ultimate value, maximum value, transfer functions and dynamic response of first and second order systems

Hardware elements of a control system with example of temperature control setup for a bioreactor; Instrumentation - final control elements and measuring devices for flow, temperature, pressure, level, and dissolved oxygen
Transfer functions of components of feedback loop - measuring devices, transmission lines, and final control elements (pneumatic control valves and control valve characteristics); Dead time processes
Types of feedback controllers; Control laws and transfer functions of P, PI, and PID controllers; Dynamic behaviour of feedback-controlled processes

Difference between open loop and closed loop control systems; Closed loop transfer function for feedback (positive and negative) processes; Servo and regulatory responses due to proportional, integral, derivative, and composite control actions
Notion of stability; Stable and unstable systems; BIBO stability
Prediction of stability of transfer function for open loop and closed loop systems; Characteristic equation
Routh Hurwitz criterion for stability; Crossover frequency, gain and phase margin; Bode stability criterion
Nyquist stability criterion; Numerical examples on Routh analysis, crossover frequency, and PM
Design of feedback controllers - semi-empirical tuning techniques (CC and ZN tuning); Numerical problems based on semi-empirical tuning rules
Introduction to other methods of controller tuning - Internal Model Control (IMC) tuning, Model Predictive Control (MPC), Fuzzy Logic PID tuning, and Adaptive PID control (basic overview only)
